### 1. **Living Room Lighting Function and Energy Consumption**

We are given the natural light function:
$$
N(t) = 300 + 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)
$$
This function represents the natural light in lumens at time $ t $ hours after sunrise.

The total illumination required is 500 lumens. The LED lights can be adjusted from 0 to 500 lumens. Therefore, the LED light output $ L(t) $ must satisfy:
$$
N(t) + L(t) = 500
$$
Solving for $ L(t) $:
$$
L(t) = 500 - N(t)
$$
$$
L(t) = 500 - \left(300 + 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)\right)
$$
$$
L(t) = 200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)
$$

Now, we calculate the total energy consumption of the LEDs over a 24-hour period. The LEDs consume 0.1 watts per lumen-hour. The total energy consumption is:
$$
\text{Energy} = \int_0^{24} L(t) \cdot 0.1 \, dt
$$
Substitute $ L(t) $:
$$
\text{Energy} = \int_0^{24} \left(200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)\right) \cdot 0.1 \, dt
$$
$$
\text{Energy} = 0.1 \int_0^{24} \left(200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)\right) \, dt
$$
$$
\text{Energy} = 0.1 \left[ 200t + 200 \int_0^{24} \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) dt \right]
$$
